body: PT Astra International Tbk (ASII) reported an 8.7% month-to-month increase in car sales to 43,596 units in September, driven by strong sales from Toyota Lexus, Daihatsu, and Isuzu, with low-cost green cars accounting for 25.3% of total sales. Despite a 2% year-on-year decline, Astra's market share increased to 56% in October 2024, with its low-cost green car market share spiking to 75%, amid a 6.2% year-on-year improvement in national car sales.
title: Astra International's Car Sales Rise 8.7% Month-on-Month in October 2024

body: PT Vale Indonesia Tbk (INCO) and Gem Co Ltd have agreed on a $1.4 billion contract for a net-zero nickel production project in Central Sulawesi, which aims to generate 60,000 tons of nickel MHP per annum using high-pressure acid leaching technology. The investment will also allocate $40 million for a research and development center, $30 million for an ESG compound, and $10 million for community public infrastructure development.
title: Vale Indonesia and Chinese firm Gem Co agree on $1.4 billion net-zero nickel production project in Central Sulawesi.

body: PT Resource Alam Indonesia Tbk (ALMI) will distribute an interim stock dividend with a ratio of 190:3, issuing 3 new shares for every 190 old shares, using 152.37 million shares from its treasury stock gained from previous buybacks. The plan will be presented for approval at the extraordinary general meeting of shareholders on December 18, 2024, and will be distributed proportionally to shareholders based on their existing holdings.
title: PT Resource Alam Indonesia to Distribute Interim Stock Dividend with 190:3 Ratio Pending Shareholder Approval.

body: Indonesia and China signed 7 strategic cooperation agreements, including a phytosanitary requirements protocol for fresh coconut exports and a memorandum of understanding on water resources cooperation, with Indonesia's President Prabowo Subianto stating that China is Indonesia's primary economic strategic partner and biggest investor. The strengthened partnership is expected to create a conducive condition in Asia, with Subianto scheduled to attend a meeting with the Indonesian Chamber of Commerce and prominent Chinese companies, involving investment agreements worth over USD 10 billion.
title: Indonesia and China Sign 7 Strategic Cooperation Agreements to Boost Bilateral Relations and Investment

body: PT Fast Food Indonesia Tbk (FAST), the KFC franchise license holder, reported a record loss of IDR 557.08 billion in Q3 2024, a 266.44% year-on-year increase, driven by declining revenue and shrinking gross profit margin. The company's revenue fell to IDR 3.59 trillion, with food and beverage sales being the largest contributor, while operating expenses increased, contributing to the widening loss.
title: KFC Franchise Holder PT Fast Food Indonesia Reports Record Loss of IDR 557.08 Billion in Q3 2024.

body: PT TBS Energi Utama Tbk (TBS) has acquired Sembcorp Environment Pte Ltd for SGD 405 million, which will be adjusted based on the settlement account date of the transaction closing. The acquisition is expected to reinforce TBS's position in regional waste management and environmental solutions, and improve its financial position, without negatively affecting its operational or financial condition.
title: TBS Energi Utama acquires Sembcorp Environment Pte Ltd for SGD 405 million.

body: Foreign investors purchased 1.31 billion shares of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) with a net volume of 495.60 million shares, followed by PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BUMI) with 335.06 million shares and a net volume of 110.57 million shares. On the other hand, foreign investors sold shares of banking sector companies, including PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI), PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), with net volumes of -86.07 million, -85.36 million, and -64.15 million shares, respectively.
title: Foreign investors' net sales in Indonesian market reach 215.52 million shares on Friday, with digital corporation PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk being the most bought stock.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 996.75 million shares, with the largest sales in PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) at 1.40 billion shares, PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BUMI) at 759.39 million shares, and banking sector shares, including PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA). Meanwhile, the largest purchases were made in PT MNC Land Tbk (KPIG) at 400.80 million shares, PT Aneka Tambang Tbk (ANTM) at 88.35 million shares, and PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPI) at 58.26 million shares.
title: Foreign investors sell majority of shares in Indonesian companies, led by PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia TBK.

body: PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa Tbk (KMDs) has announced an interim dividend distribution of IDR 15 per share, totaling IDR 12 billion, to be paid on November 29, 2024, to shareholders listed as of November 19, 2024. The company reported a net profit of IDR 40.44 billion in the first nine months of 2024, with retained earnings of IDR 101.72 billion and total equity of IDR 218.11 billion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa announces IDR 15 per share interim dividend, totaling IDR 12 billion.

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BNI) has expanded its mobile banking application, Wondr BNI, to major cities worldwide, including Hong Kong, and has seen an 81% year-over-year increase in mobile banking transactions from Indonesian migrant workers, reaching 2 million transactions by the end of 2023. BNI has also received the "Best Indonesian Bank in Hong Kong Serving Indonesian Diaspora" award from the Consulate General of the Republic of Indonesia in Hong Kong, recognizing its commitment to providing innovative financial solutions to the Indonesian diaspora.
title: BNI's Wondr Mobile Banking Expands to Hong Kong, Supports Indonesia Week 2024 with Financial Literacy and Banking Solutions.

body: The Indonesian government is set to auction off 8 series of government securities on November 14, 2024, with an indicative target of IDR 22 trillion, comprising 2 series of treasury notes and 6 series of government bonds with coupons ranging from 6.5% to 7.12%. This will be the 21st SUN auction this year, with the government having received a total of IDR 459.56 trillion from the previous 20 auctions since the beginning of 2024.
title: Indonesian government to auction 8 series of government securities worth IDR 22 trillion on November 14.

body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals TBK (BRMS) acquired 80 million shares, increasing its stake in the company from 5.34% to 5.4%, while PT Sinar Mas Cakrawala added 2.17 million shares in a financial services provider. Meanwhile, ownership percentages of foreign investors in PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK (GOTO) increased, with SVF GT Subco Pte Ltd-270034000 and Taobao China Holding Limited holding 7.65% and 7.43% stakes, respectively.
title: Multiple investors and companies adjust their stakes in various Indonesian firms across different sectors.

body: PT Wijaya Cahaya Timber Tbk (FWCT) has announced an interim dividend distribution of IDR 3 per share, totaling IDR 5.63 billion, to be paid on December 4, 2024, to shareholders listed as of November 20, 2024. The company reported a net profit of IDR 43.52 billion in the first nine months of 2024 and had retained earnings of IDR 136.3 billion and total equity of IDR 314.73 billion as of September 2024.
title: PT Wijaya Cahaya Timber to Distribute IDR 5.63 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: Foreign investors purchased 4.67 billion shares, resulting in a net foreign volume surplus of 181.96 million shares, with top buys including PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BUMI) with 882.48 million shares and PT Media Nusantara Citra Tbk (MNCN) with 66.19 million shares. Meanwhile, foreign investors sold 4.49 billion shares, with notable sales including PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with 137.77 million shares, PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) with 120.21 million shares, and PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BBNI) with 39.79 million shares.
title: Foreign investors purchase 4.67 billion shares in Indonesian market, led by mineral mining and renewable energy sectors.

body: Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves in October 2024 reached USD 151.2 billion, up from USD 149.9 billion in September 2024, equivalent to 6.6 months of import or 6.4 months of government foreign debt repayment. The increase in forex reserves was driven by tax income and foreign debt withdrawal, and Bank Indonesia believes the current reserves will maintain external sector resiliency, supporting positive export prospects and attractive investment returns.
title: Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves reach USD 151.2 billion in October 2024, exceeding international adequacy standards.

body: PT XL Axiata Tbk (EXCL) reported a net profit of IDR 1.32 trillion in the first nine months of 2024, a 31.7% year-over-year increase from IDR 999.99 billion in the same period of 2023, driven by a 6.24% revenue growth to IDR 25.36 trillion. The company's total assets stood at IDR 85.17 trillion, with cash and cash equivalents of IDR 1.83 trillion and equity of IDR 25.7 trillion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT XL Axiata reports 31.7% year-on-year net profit growth to IDR 1.32 trillion in the first nine months of 2024.
